questions.question: Economics is best defined as:
options: The study of how scarce resources are allocated to satisfy unlimited wants

questions.question: The basic economic problem is:
Which of the following is a factor of production?
The law of demand states that:
options: As price rises, quantity demanded falls, ceteris paribus

questions.question: A normal good is one whose demand:
options: Is not affected by income changes

questions.question: Which of the following shifts the demand curve to the right?
options: A fall in the price of the good
A rise in the price of the good
A rise in consumer income (for normal goods)
An increase in the price of a complementary good

questions.question: Price elasticity of demand measures the:
options: Change in supply due to a price change
Responsiveness of quantity demanded to a change in price
Change in income due to price changes
Responsiveness of supply to income changes

Total cost equals:
Monopoly is a market structure characterised by:
options: A single seller with no close substitutes

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of money?
Inflation is defined as:
options: A fall in the general price level
A sustained rise in the general price level

questions.question: The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) measures:
options: The total value of goods and services produced in a country in a year

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a public good?
The central bank of Nigeria is:
An increase in government spending, all things being equal, will:
options: Have no effect on national income

questions.question: Division of labour refers to:
Which of the following is a merit of division of labour?
The opportunity cost of a decision is:
options: The money cost of the decision
The total cost of all alternatives

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of perfect competition?
options: Many buyers and sellers with homogeneous products

questions.question: Direct tax is a tax paid:
options: Directly by the person on whom it is levied

questions.question: Balance of trade refers to:
options: The difference between a country's total exports and imports of goods
The difference between income and expenditure

questions.question: Which of the following correctly defines a merit good?
options: A good provided only by the private sector
A good the government provides because the market underproduces it
A good with no positive externalities

questions.question: Oligopoly is a market structure with:
The multiplier effect refers to:
options: The effect of taxation on savings
The magnified effect of an initial change in spending on national income
The effect of interest rates on investment
The effect of exchange rates on exports

questions.question: Which of the following is a contractionary fiscal policy?
options: Increasing government spending and reducing taxes
Reducing government spending and raising taxes

questions.question: The quantity theory of money states that:
options: High money supply always reduces inflation

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of structural unemployment?
options: A teacher who cannot find work during school holidays
A factory worker made redundant due to automation
A new graduate searching for first job

questions.question: The law of diminishing marginal utility states that:
options: As consumption increases, total utility always falls
As more units are consumed, each additional unit gives less additional satisfaction

questions.question: Foreign exchange rate is the:
options: Price of one currency in terms of another
Rate at which central banks lend to commercial banks

questions.question: Which of the following is a source of government revenue in Nigeria?
Population census is important because it:
options: Provides data for economic planning and resource allocation

questions.question: Barter trade refers to:
options: Exchange of goods for goods without money

questions.question: Which of the following is a pull factor of rural-urban migration?
options: Drought and famine in rural areas
Lack of social amenities in rural areas

questions.question: The IS-LM model analyses the relationship between:
Which of the following is an indirect tax?
An inferior good is one whose demand:
Which of the following best defines economic development?
options: A sustained improvement in the standard of living, reduction in poverty, and structural transformation of the economy

questions.question: The Phillips Curve illustrates the relationship between:
Which of the following is a feature of oligopoly?
Devaluation of a currency means:
options: An increase in the value of the currency
A reduction in the official value of a currency relative to others

questions.question: Which of the following is a consequence of inflation?
options: Redistribution of income from debtors to creditors

questions.question: The Lorenz curve is used to measure:
Which of the following is an objective of ECOWAS?
options: Promotion of economic integration and cooperation in West Africa
Creation of a single currency only
Control of oil prices in Africa

questions.question: Perfect price discrimination means a monopolist charges:
options: The same price to all consumers
Each consumer their maximum willingness to pay

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT included in calculating GDP by expenditure method?
The term 'liquidity trap' refers to a situation where:
options: Banks have no money to lend
Monetary policy becomes ineffective because people hoard money

questions.question: Which of the following is a consequence of a favourable balance of payments?
Consumer surplus is the difference between:
options: The price paid and the cost of production
What a consumer is willing to pay and what they actually pay

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes a progressive tax?
options: The tax rate increases as income increases
The tax rate decreases as income increases

questions.question: The concept of comparative advantage states that:
options: A country should produce only what it is best at
A country should produce goods in which it has the lowest opportunity cost
Rich countries should help poor countries produce
Countries should produce all goods themselves

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of the Central Bank of Nigeria?
Stagflation refers to:
options: High unemployment and high inflation occurring simultaneously

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a determinant of the elasticity of demand?
options: Proportion of income spent on the good

questions.question: The Gini coefficient measures:
Which of the following is a characteristic of developing economies like Nigeria?
options: High dependence on primary commodity exports

questions.question: Monetary policy involves the use of:
options: Interest rates and money supply by the central bank

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a negative externality?
options: A beautiful garden that neighbours enjoy

questions.question: The break-even point is where:
options: Total revenue equals total variable cost
Total revenue equals total cost (zero profit/loss)

questions.question: Which of the following is a tool of monetary policy?
The concept of economies of scale refers to:
options: Cost advantages gained as output increases

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a mixed economy?
options: An economy with only private sector firms
An economy with only state-owned enterprises
An economy combining private and public sector resource allocation
An economy based solely on agriculture

questions.question: The Human Development Index (HDI) measures:
options: A combination of life expectancy, education, and income

questions.question: Which of the following is a cause of market failure?
Game theory is most relevant to the study of:
The crowding out effect suggests that high government borrowing:
options: Reduces interest rates and stimulates investment
Raises interest rates and reduces private investment
Has no effect on private investment

questions.question: Which of the following is the main goal of the World Trade Organisation (WTO)?
options: To provide loans to developing countries
To promote free and fair international trade

questions.question: Privatisation refers to:
options: Transfer of ownership from public to private sector

questions.question: Which of the following is a cause of unemployment in Nigeria?
options: Rapid technological advancement without matching skills

questions.question: The term 'Dutch Disease' refers to:
options: The negative impact of resource booms on other sectors of the economy

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of commercial banks?
options: Setting interest rates for the economy

questions.question: The concept of sustainable development means:
options: Development that meets present needs without compromising future generations' ability to meet their needs
Reducing economic growth to protect the environment

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the labour force?
options: All people of working age who are employed or actively seeking employment

questions.question: The J-curve effect describes:
options: The relationship between inflation and unemployment
The initial worsening then improvement of trade balance after currency devaluation
The impact of interest rates on investment
